paper_title: Enhancing TiO₂-based dye-sensitized solar cell performance using Br₃⁻/I⁻ electrolyte and natural dyes from pomegranate and teak leaves

The teak leaf-based cell reached a PCE of 0.0373% under the same conditions. In contrast, cells with the conventional | 4 mA cm⁻², fill factor of 0.409, and a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 0.0788%. The teak leaf-based cell reached a PCE of 0.0373% under the same conditions. In contrast, cells with the conventional I₃⁻/I⁻ electrolyte yielded lower efficiencies of 0. | ate and teak leaf dyes using Br₃⁻/I⁻ and I₃⁻/I⁻ redox electrolytes The DSSCs sensitized with pomegranate dye achieved a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 0.0788% using the Br₃⁻/I⁻ electrolyte, compared to 0.0255% with the conventional I₃⁻/I⁻ system. Similarly, the teak leaf dye se | yte, compared to 0.0255% with the conventional I₃⁻/I⁻ system. Similarly, the teak leaf dye sensitized cell exhibited an efficiency of 0.0373% with Br₃⁻/I⁻, which decreased to 0.0108% with I₃⁻/I⁻.
